generatedBy: scripts/build_ui_contract_manifest.py -- DO NOT HAND-EDIT, re-run the script instead
generatedFrom: harness/models/synty/ (dimensions measured directly from the promoted PNG files)

entries.notes: REVISION (steel art-direction, see $steelRevisionNote): clean square beveled silver/steel frame, straight edges -- was Frame_Box21_Variant01 (gold). General dialog/panel chrome -- default pick for the #525 dock/panel shell primitive.

REVISION (steel art-direction, see $steelRevisionNote): bright chrome/silver frame with diamond-cut corners, larger insets -- was Frame_Box18_Variant01 (gold, diamond side-spike brackets). For emphasis surfaces (e.g. victory/defeat modal border, hero ability card) rather than everyday chrome. Frame_Box20 (steel, matching Box18's side-spike ornament more closely) was tried first but rejected -- its single bottom-center diamond notch visibly distorts under a 3x-wide stretch; Box23's cut corners stayed crisp in both 3x-wide and 3x-tall renders.

REVISION (steel art-direction, see $steelRevisionNote): steel-blue member of the same Box_Small01/02/03 shape family -- was Frame_Box_Small01 (gold). Compact frame (256x256 source, smaller absolute insets than frame-panel but same proportional bevel weight) sized for action-bar-button-scale chrome.
Dark steel bezel/trough, transparent interior. Generic HP-style bar track -- pairs with bar-fill-hp or bar-fill-resource composited into the interior inset region.

REVISION (steel art-direction, see $steelRevisionNote): solid silver/gray gradient fill, tapered diamond end-caps -- was Bar_Horizontal04 (gold); swapped with bar-fill-resource. No red/crimson fill exists anywhere in the library (checked), so silver is the steel-direction stand-in for the conventional HP-red read. Clip (do not stretch) to represent percentage; cap margin matches bar-track-hp's interior inset so the fill sits flush inside the bezel.

REVISION (steel art-direction, see $steelRevisionNote): solid gold gradient fill, same shape as bar-fill-hp -- was Bar_Horizontal05 (silver); swapped with bar-fill-hp. The bars family only has these 2 solid fill sprites in the whole pack, so gold remains here as the deliberately-distinct secondary-resource color (stamina/spell-slots/etc.) now that HP has taken silver -- this is the one warm-metal-toned sprite left in the contract, chosen specifically so HP and the secondary resource still read as visually distinct gauges sharing bar-track-hp.

Reuses the #490 action-bar rage icon path (dumbbell glyph) -- no copy. Judgment call: matches the action button rather than pulling a separate status-icon-set glyph (icons/status Status_AttackUp01 crossed-swords was the alternative); flagged for veto.
Reuses the #490 action-bar dodge icon path (shield glyph) -- no copy. Same judgment call as badge-raging: button/badge visual consistency over a distinct glyph.

Skull glyph from the #473 status-icon set. Covers both 'unconscious' and 'dying' (0 HP / death-save territory) as a single badge per the #467 checklist -- there is no separate sleep/Zzz unconscious-only glyph in the promoted set.
Generic upward-arrow glyph from the #473 status-icon set. Generic positive-effect badge, not tied to a specific buff.
Generic downward-arrow glyph from the #473 status-icon set. Generic negative-effect badge, not tied to a specific debuff.
Rank-and-file goblin portrait for enemy initiative slots.
PLACEHOLDER -- Goblin WarChief standing in for a generic 'boss' tier portrait until a dedicated boss portrait set exists. See $placeholderNote.

REVISION (steel art-direction, see $steelRevisionNote): bright silver/chrome title-plate frame with flowing wing/leaf-shaped scrollwork, text-free (empty interior for app-rendered text) -- was Banner07 (warm gold celtic-knot scrollwork). A genuine steel/silver celebratory-shaped option exists in this pack (unlike the original judgment call, which assumed none did), so no gold accent remains anywhere in the chrome/banner surfaces.
Cold steel title-plate frame with hook/claw corner ornaments, text-free. Judgment call -- see $judgmentCallNote.
